title: Survival live loop writes no periodic heartbeat log line (dashboard log panel appears empty on a healthy quiet service)
source: CEO observation 2026-05-30: dashboard log panel empty + logs/survival_service.log has only the startup banner. Diagnosed as by-design quiet logging (logs per daily cycle + alarms only; 60s risk ticks emit no line).
why: The interleaved loop only logs per daily CYCLE (rebalance) and on alarms; the 60s fast-risk ticks append equity + snapshot but emit no log line. On a quiet weekend (no cycle, no alarm) the log stays at 1 line and the dashboard's log_tail (webapp/state_reader.py ~line 215) shows nothing — indistinguishable at a glance from a dead service.
value_rationale: Low: purely observability ergonomics; the service is verifiably alive via heartbeat mtime + intraday equity marks. But a periodic 'alive' line removes the 'is it dead?' doubt.
scope: Emit a throttled heartbeat log line every N minutes (e.g. 15m) from run_loop: '[hb] alive, equity=..., cursor=..., dd_latched=...'.
Throttle so it does not spam (one line per N min, not per 60s tick).
Ensure it does not interfere with the armed risk path (pure logging).
recommendation: Cheap addition in scripts/run_survival_live.py run_loop; ship alongside or after B0104 step 5.

history.reason: feat(execution): B0104 step 5 â€” bounded equity curve (rotation + rollup)

Fixed: added periodic [heartbeat] print in run_loop every 10 ticks (~10 min at 60s interval). Fires at tick=0 (startup confirmation) then every 10 ticks. Guarded by on_risk is None so it does not fire in test callbacks. 1110 tests pass.
fix(ops): B0106 heartbeat log + webapp equity/DD fixes
